title: Can dissolving filler with hyaluronidase cause loose skin in other areas of the body 🤔？ Im not too s
filename: Can dissolving filler with hyaluronidase cause loose skin in other areas of the body 🤔？ Im not too s.en.vtt
excerpt: Skin hanging from the bones it's all loose my skin isn't attached to my face what is this it's all just hanging the removal of facial fillers that some claim has impacted their whole body see my skin kind of looks loose when I think about it in a certain light um and this for me …

segments.text: skin hanging from the bones it's all loose my skin isn't attached to my face what is this it's all just hanging the removal of facial fillers that some claim has impacted their whole body see my skin kind of looks loose when I think about it in a certain light um and this for me is one of the biggest issues with this particular video which is it's very hard as a clinician to say that those patients are wrong but at the same time there's definitely room for them to be wrong because I know when you look at your face with fear which is a lot of what a good clinician understands patients do about any issue you can often pull out particular problems it doesn't make a lot of sense to me that you'd be able to get baggy skin in your legs from having a little bit of high lays in your face which is what this video shows and although it's unregulated and they say untested that's only for use for dissolving dermal

filler this medication has been around since the 50s I think used in medicine to dissolve or to allow drugs to disperse so although it is untested for a specific indication it has been used in the human body before then it's only since aesthetic patients have been having lots of it that we've had more of this discussion around with whether it's causing a secondary problem I don't see a lot of evidence that there's something certain going on here of course will pay attention because that's what a good clinician does but we need a lot more data and information before we make the conclusion that this is really causing the problems that you see in the patients in this video

keyPoints.text: Filler this medication has been around since the 50s I think used in medicine to dissolve or to allow drugs to disperse so although it is untested for a specific indication it has been used in the human body before then …

transcript: skin hanging from the bones it's all loose my skin isn't attached to my face what is this it's all just hanging the removal of facial fillers that some claim has impacted their whole body see my skin kind of looks loose when I think about it in a certain light um and this for me is one of the biggest issues with this particular video which is it's very hard as a clinician to say that those patients are wrong but at the same time there's definitely room for them to be wrong because I know when you look at your face with fear which is a lot of what a good clinician understands patients do about any issue you can often pull out particular problems it doesn't make a lot of sense to me that you'd be able to get baggy skin in your legs from having a little bit of high lays in your face which is what this video shows and although it's unregulated and they say untested that's only for use for dissolving dermal filler this medication has been around since the 50s I think used in medicine to dissolve or to allow drugs to disperse so although it is untested for a specific indication it has been used in the human body before then it's only since aesthetic patients have been having lots of it that we've had more of this discussion around with whether it's causing a secondary problem I don't see a lot of evidence that there's something certain going on here of course will pay attention because that's what a good clinician does but we need a lot more data and information before we make the conclusion that this is really causing the problems that you see in the patients in this video
